name: Entrín Bajo a la Albuera — circular por Rivera de Nogales
region_intro: Mountain biking around Entrín Bajo features a network of routes traversing the rural landscape of Extremadura. The area is characterized by rolling hills, agricultural lands, and river valleys, such as the Rivera de Nogales. Routes often connect small towns and historical sites, offering varied terrain suitable for different skill levels. The region provides a mix of moderate climbs and descents, with some longer distance mountain bike routes available. Best mountain bike trails around Entrín Bajo The most popular mountain bike trail is La Albuera a Almendral — circular, a 35.4 miles (57.0 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 23 minutes to complete. This route offers a comprehensive tour through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Entrín Bajo a la Albuera — circular por Rivera de Nogales, a moderate 19.2 miles (30.9 km) path. This route follows the Rivera de Nogales, providing scenic views along the river valley. Local mountain bikers also love the Nogales Castle loop from Santa Marta de los Barros, a 28.7 miles (46.2 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Mountain biking around Entrín Bajo is defined by open rural landscapes, river valleys, and connecting routes between small towns. The network offers options for easy mountain bike trails as well as more challenging long distance mountain bike routes. The routes in Entrín Bajo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 15 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Entrín Bajo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Entrín Bajo?
A: There are 13 mountain bike trails available around Entrín Bajo. These routes traverse the rural landscape of Extremadura, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Entrín Bajo?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail around Entrín Bajo, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. The region's rolling hills and agricultural lands provide a gentle introduction to mountain biking.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Yes, for advanced riders seeking a challenge, there are 2 difficult mountain bike routes available around Entrín Bajo. These trails offer more demanding terrain and longer distances.

Q: What are some options for long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: For longer rides, consider routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358514381/la-albuera-a-almendral-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Albuera a Almendral — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 35.4 miles (57.0 km). Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Torre de Miguel Sesmero&lt;/a&gt;, covering 48.1 km.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike trails around Entrín Bajo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in the area are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359937147/entrin-bajo-a-la-albuera-circular-por-rivera-de-nogales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrín Bajo a la Albuera — circular por Rivera de Nogales&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle loop from Santa Marta de los Barros&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Entrín Bajo?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Entrín Bajo is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in Extremadura, and winters can be cooler and wetter, though often still rideable.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain around Entrín Bajo is characterized by rolling hills, agricultural lands, and river valleys, particularly along the Rivera de Nogales. You can expect a mix of moderate climbs and descents, with routes often connecting small towns and historical sites.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Entrín Bajo?
A: The mountain bike trails around Entrín Bajo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the open rural landscapes, the scenic river valleys, and the well-connected routes between small towns.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that follow river valleys?
A: Yes, the region features river valleys such as the Rivera de Nogales. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359937147/entrin-bajo-a-la-albuera-circular-por-rivera-de-nogales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrín Bajo a la Albuera — circular por Rivera de Nogales&lt;/a&gt; specifically follows this river, providing scenic views along the way.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by historical sites or landmarks?
A: The routes in Entrín Bajo often connect small towns and historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle loop from Santa Marta de los Barros&lt;/a&gt; leads through varied rural landscapes and passes by the historic Nogales Castle.

Q: What should I wear for mountain biking in Entrín Bajo?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for sun exposure, it's advisable to wear appropriate cycling attire, including a helmet, padded shorts, moisture-wicking layers, and sturdy cycling shoes. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during warmer months.
tips: This quiet town of less than 600 inhabitants is a good place to stop and enjoy one of the two squares it has.

In 1811, in the War of Independence, the famous battle of La Albuera took place. As a reminder of that battle, you can see, on Avenida de Extremadura, two large mosaics commemorating it, which was the beginning of the end of the French invasion of Spanish territory.
